message: slice 3b: owner-aware Visibility (Public/Owner(id)/All) derived once in resolve_visibility (src/http/api.rs), threaded through all read surfaces (documents, links/backlinks/graph, RAG chunks, garden embeds, /ask, /related). read-scoped author sees only own drafts + published; admin sees all; public site pinned to Public. No TOCTOU. Tests: scoped_tokens_slice3b.rs. Docs synced (ROUTER, plan 023). Shipped via PR #30.

Linear board reconciled with repo. Closed 10 shipped issues verified in code: M1 Authoring MVP (CIL-114..118 — author CLI init/push/publish, front-matter parse, docs) and M2 Identity/Scopes/Audit (CIL-119..123 — migrations, scoped token auth, ownership enforce, audit events, token operator cmds). Board was stale: shipped work sat in Todo. Filed 3 untracked repo items as new issues: CIL-197 (plan 024 stored-chunks /related, P1), CIL-198 (plan 025 embedding provider provenance, P2), CIL-199 (ADR 0010 browser login UI). Open board now = v0.2 hardening/docs/ops cluster + 197/198/199. Dups CIL-140/141/142 left in Duplicate state.

Plan 024 (stored-chunk /related, CIL-197) and plan 025 (embedding provider provenance, CIL-198) were already implemented and merged to main before this task — 024 via PR #23 (commit 2bb53e5), 025 via PR #26 (commit 69c4b09). Verified live: related_notes_for_note() in src/db/chunks.rs sources neighbors from note_chunks (min cosine distance, no body re-embed); document_related in src/http/ai.rs no longer embeds the body; provenance columns (embedding_provider/model) gate cross-provider compares; 4 related_ tests present incl related_does_not_call_embedder. Branch HEAD == origin/main (bf96eb5), zero code to write. Plan drift check correctly flagged a STOP. Coordinator chose doc-sync: marked plans/README.md rows 024 and 025 TODO->DONE. No behaviour change, so ROUTER Current Project State left untouched.

CIL-125: request correlation IDs. New src/http/request_id.rs from_fn middleware honours well-formed inbound X-Request-Id (charset/len validated to block header/log injection) else mints UUID v4; stashes id in a tokio task_local; echoes X-Request-Id on every response; enriches the TraceLayer span via make_span_with so every log line carries request_id (no duplicate request logging); error envelope (src/error.rs ErrorPayload) gains requestId read from the task-local so a user-reported error traces to logs. Middleware is OUTERMOST (added after TraceLayer in router.rs) so id is in scope at span build. Tests: tests/request_id_contract.rs (generated/echoed/error-body+header-match/malformed-replaced) + unit tests in request_id.rs. fmt+clippy+test all green.

CIL-128: pragmatic write rate limiting. governor GCRA from_fn middleware (src/http/rate_limit.rs) throttles mutations (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) + /ask, bucketed by authenticated credential (sha256 of x-api-key, no DB hit) else client IP (forwarded header then ConnectInfo peer). INKWELL_WRITE_RATE_LIMIT req/min, default 60, 0 disables; 429 + Retry-After. Reads + public HTML never throttled. Layered inside security_headers, outside handlers; main.rs serves with into_make_service_with_connect_info. Reverses prior audit stance (urgency blunted by 256 KiB body cap + constant-time key compare) — body cap bounds size not count, ct-compare hardens auth not write floods; with scoped tokens as write surface + public /ask this is the missing cheap control. Chose governor direct over tower_governor (can't skip GET on any() routes). New AppError::TooManyRequests, Config.write_rate_limit, tests/rate_limit_contract.rs + unit tests. Docs: .env.example, README, ROUTER, decisions, patterns/add-middleware.md. cargo fmt/clippy/test --all green; cargo audit unavailable in env.

CIL-128 hardening (PR #36 review — Macroscope HIGH + Copilot): rate-limit keying now VALIDATES the principal via authenticate() BEFORE bucketing instead of hashing the raw credential. Closes a DoS bypass where an anon caller on /ask or /webmention sent a different random x-api-key/inkwell_session per request to mint a fresh bucket each time, evade the per-IP limit, and grow the limiter map unboundedly. Invalid/forged credential -> no principal -> client-IP bucket; valid principal -> p:<author-id>. Forwarded-header trust (X-Forwarded-For/X-Real-IP) for the IP path is now opt-in via INKWELL_TRUST_FORWARDED_HEADERS (default off) so a directly-exposed instance can't be spoofed; off keys by the real ConnectInfo peer. RateLimitState::new now takes (Arc<Config>, PgPool); keying is async (DB-validates tokens/sessions). Trades the no-DB-hit hot path for correctness (writes are low-volume; disabled-mode short-circuits before any auth/key work). New regression test invalid_api_keys_cannot_bypass_the_limiter + client_ip trust-gate unit test. Amends the CIL-128 decision above.

decision: Introduced SiteMeta<'a> struct in src/views/layout.rs carrying name/description/author/base_url/custom_css_url; constructed from Config via SiteMeta::from_config in every handler; passed as first arg to all view render functions replacing the old site_url: Option<&str> parameter. Added 4 env vars: INKWELL_SITE_TITLE, INKWELL_SITE_DESCRIPTION, INKWELL_SITE_AUTHOR, INKWELL_CUSTOM_CSS_URL. Feed and JSON-LD also use these values. No DB migration required.
why: Operators need to brand the public site without editing source code. A single SiteMeta struct avoids proliferating individual parameters across all view functions.

decision: Archive browsing via /archive and /archive/{year}/{month}; prev/next doc nav on document pages; both degrade gracefully on DB failure; /archive added to sitemap; no DB migration needed (EXTRACT on existing created_at column)

message: CIL-129: shareable draft preview links. New migration 0022 creates preview_tokens table (document_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, prefix UNIQUE, token_hash SHA-256, optional expires_at/revoked_at). Token format: pvw_<12-hex-prefix>_<64-hex-secret>, generate_preview() + parse_preview_prefix() in src/domain/token.rs. DB layer: src/db/preview_tokens.rs (insert/find/list/revoke). Routes: GET|POST /documents/{slug}/preview-tokens (write scope + ownership), DELETE /documents/{slug}/preview-tokens/{prefix} (revoke), GET /documents/{slug}/preview?token=<pvw_...> (no-auth read-only). Preview handler does: parse prefix -> DB lookup -> constant-time hash compare -> revoked/expired check -> fetch document (StatusFilter::None, bypasses draft gate) -> document_id match. Any failure returns 401 (never 404) so callers cannot determine whether the document exists. Normal public GET is unchanged; drafts stay hidden. Tests: tests/preview_contract.rs covers positive access, cross-doc token rejection, revocation, expiry, auth gates on management surface, method guards. cargo fmt/clippy/test --all green.

CIL-130: media asset workflow complete. API (POST /media, GET /media/{id}) and migration 0019 already shipped in PR #28. Added: inkwell author upload <file> CLI command (mime_from_path infers type from extension; calls InkwellClient::upload_media; prints URL + Markdown reference). Docs updated: DEPLOYMENT.md gets media storage section (bytea in Postgres, no external store, capacity planning note, backup note); BACKUP-RESTORE.md migration list bumped to 0022 + media blob note; architecture.md stale 'no file uploads' line corrected. Storage model: local DB (bytea), v1 conservative choice — no S3/object-store dependency.
